response:
Use this minimal reproducible flow:

```bash
# fresh setup (normal writable machine)
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade requests
```

```bash
# hello-world style verification call
python3 -c $'import requests\nfrom requests.adapters import BaseAdapter\nfrom requests.models import Response\nprint(\"requests_version\", requests.__version__)\nclass HelloAdapter(BaseAdapter):\n    def send(self, request, **kwargs):\n        resp = Response(); resp.status_code = 200; resp._content = b\"hello-world\"; resp.url = request.url; resp.request = request; resp.headers[\"Content-Type\"] = \"text/plain\"; return resp\n    def close(self):\n        pass\ns = requests.Session(); s.mount(\"hello://\", HelloAdapter()); r = s.get(\"hello://demo\")\nprint(\"status\", r.status_code)\nprint(\"body\", r.text)\nprint(\"url\", r.url)\nprint(\"content_type\", r.headers.get(\"Content-Type\"))'
```

Verified output from execution (exit code `0`):

```text
requests_version 2.32.5
status 200
body hello-world
url hello://demo
content_type text/plain
```

This confirms a working minimal `requests` setup and successful basic hello-style call.
